description_long: This object is a pair of tactical knives with a modern, minimalist design, featuring a full tang construction. The blades are straight-edged with a sharp, pointed tip, and the spine has a series of jimping notches for enhanced grip and control. The handles are skeletonized, with multiple circular cutouts and a pronounced finger groove for ergonomic handling. The knives are made entirely of metal, likely stainless steel, with a matte black finish that reduces glare and increases durability. There are no visible handle scales or coverings, emphasizing a lightweight and utilitarian build. The overall appearance is sleek and functional, suitable for utility or self-defense purposes in an urban environment.
description: A pair of modern, skeletonized tactical knives with straight, pointed blades and matte black metal construction. The handles feature circular cutouts and ergonomic grooves for grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of both knives, highlighting the straight blades, pointed tips, and skeletonized handles with circular cutouts.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thinness of the blades and the jimping along the spine, with minimal handle width visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, again showing the blade profiles and handle cutouts, but from the reverse side.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side of view 1, emphasizing the thickness and flatness of the knives.
attribute_handle_design: skeletonized, contoured with finger groove, multiple circular cutouts, matte metal
